content: Transition metals are known to make interstitial compounds. Formation of interstitial compounds makes the transition metal
explanation: Though the chemical properties do not change, when Transition metals form interstitial compounds they become more hard.

content:
Assertion (A): Zr and Hf are of almost similar atomic radii.

Reason (R): This is due to lanthanoid contraction.

options.content: Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true and Reason ( $R$ ) is the correct explanation of the Assertion (A).
Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true, but Reason ( $R$ ) is not the correct explanation of Assertion (A).
Assertion (A) is true, but Reason (R) is false.
Assertion (A) is false, but Reason (R) is true.

explanation: Both Zr and Hf have same atomic radii and this is due to the lanthanoid contraction.

content: Which property of transition metals enables them to behave as catalysts?
explanation: Because of variable oxidation state they have tendency to form bonds with many elements.

content:
Attempt any five of the following:

(a) $\mathrm{Ce}(\mathrm{III})$ is easily oxidised to $\mathrm{Ce}(\mathrm{IV})$. Comment.

(b) $\mathrm{E}^{\circ}\left(\mathrm{Mn}^{2+} / \mathrm{Mn}\right)$ is -1.18 V . Why is this value highly negative in comparison to neighbouring $d$ block elements?

(c) Which element of $3 d$ series has lowest enthalpy of atomisation and why?

(d) What happens when sodium chromate is acidified?

(e) $\mathrm{Zn}, \mathrm{Cd}$ and Hg are soft metals. Why?

(f) Why is permanganate titration not carried out in the presence of HCl ?

(g) The lower oxides of transition metals are basic whereas the highest are amphoteric/acidic. Give reason.
explanation:
(a) Ce , having the configuration of $4 f^1 5 d^1 6 s^2$.

Ce (III) will have the configuration: $4 f^1 5 d^0 6 s^0$, it can easily lose an electron to acquire the stable configuration of $4 f^0 5 d^0 6 s^0$.

This loss of an electron transforms it into $\mathrm{Ce}(\mathrm{IV})$, which has the noble gas configuration of xenon (Xe).

(b) The electronic configuration of Mn :

Mn $1 s^2 2 s^2 2 p^6 3 s^2 3 p^6 3 d^5 4 s^2$

$\mathrm{Mn}^{+2}$ has a half-filled state of $3 d^5$.

Negative $\mathrm{E}^{\circ}$ values of $\mathrm{Mn}^{2+} / \mathrm{Mn}$ is because of the stabilities of half-filled $\left(3 d^5: \mathrm{Mn}^{2+}\right)$ configuration.

(c) The greater the number of unpaired electrons, the stronger is the resultant bonding and hence, higher is the enthalpy of atomisation. In the $3 d$ series, from Sc to $\mathrm{Zn}, \mathrm{Zn}$ has no unpaired electrons. Hence, it exhibits weak interactions and thus possesses the least enthalpy of atomisation.

(d) At acidic pH , sodium chromate converts to sodium dichromate:

$$2 \mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{CrO}_4+2 \mathrm{HCl} \rightarrow \mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7+2 \mathrm{NaCl}+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$$

(e) Zinc, cadmium and mercury have a fully filled $d$-subshell, due to which metallic bonds are weak. That's why they are soft and have low melting point.

(f) Permanganate titration is not carried out in the presence of hydrochloric acid because some of the hydrochloric acid gets oxidised to chlorine gas. Hence, we do not get the correct endpoint for any given titration.

(g) In low oxidation state of the metal, some of the valence electrons of the metal atom are not involved in bonding. Hence it can donate electrons and behave as a base. On the other hand, in higher oxidation state of the metal, valence electrons are involved in bonding and are not available. , effective nuclear charge is high and hence it can accept electrons and behave as an acid.

content:
(a) Why is chemistry of actinoids complicated as compared to lanthanoids?

(b) Complete the following reaction and justify that it is a disproportionation reaction:

$$3 \mathrm{MnO}_4^{2-}+4 \mathrm{H}^{+} \rightarrow$$________ $$+$$ _________ $$+$$ $$\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$$.

(c) The given graph shows the trends in melting points of transition metals:

Explain the reason why $$\mathrm{Cr}$$ has highest melting point and manganese $$(\mathrm{Mn})$$ a lower melting point.
explanation:
(a) Actinoids are present below the series of lanthanoid. They are radio active in nature. To study them is more complicated as compared to lanthanoid. Second, they have variable oxidation state.

(b) $$3 \mathrm{MnO}_4^{-}+4 \mathrm{H}+\rightarrow 2 \mathrm{MnO}_4^{-}+\mathrm{MnO}_2+2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$$

It is disproportination reaction because oxidation take place in acidic medium and oxidation numbers are

$$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{MnO}_4^{2-}=6 \\ & \mathrm{MnO}_4^{-}=7 \\ & \mathrm{MnO}_2=4 \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{aligned} \text{(c)} \quad & \mathrm{Cr}=24[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 \mathrm{~d} 54 \mathrm{~s}^1 4 \\ & \mathrm{Mn}=25[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 \mathrm{~d}^5 4 \mathrm{~s}^2 \end{aligned}$$

$$\mathrm{Cr}$$ has maximum number of unpaired electrons in $$\alpha$$-orbital. So it can have maximum pairing and form bond. Number of unpaired electrons are six in $$\mathrm{Cr}$$. But Mn has five unpaired electron in d-orbital which is less than Cr. That's why boiling point of $$\mathrm{Cr}$$ is maximum in transition metal. $$\mathrm{Mn}$$ is half filled which have extra stability and less tendency of form bonds.
